title: Save 55% on 12 months of Norton VPN coverage pay just $49.99 for complete protection from scams, malware, and nefarious ads
summary: Get complete protection from scams, malware, and ads with Norton VPN's 12-month subscription. Save 55% with a discounted price of $49.99. Enjoy secure browsing and streaming, perfect for events like the World Cup.
insight: Protect your online identity and data with robust VPN coverage.

title: Anthropic warns Claude AI is building itself faster than expected, calls for option to halt frontier development 'recursive self improvement' increases risk humans lose control of AI
summary: Anthropic warns that AI's recursive self-improvement may lead to loss of human control. The company's report highlights a concerning trend where AI development is accelerating beyond expectations. This could result in unforeseen consequences for humanity.
insight: AI's exponential growth may outpace human capacity to contain it.

title: Tom's Hardware Unfiltered: Computex 2026, Day 4 the B2B shift, and we say farewell to Taipei
summary: Our team reflects on Computex 2026, highlighting key takeaways and trends. The B2B shift was a notable theme, with many exhibitors focusing on enterprise solutions. We bid farewell to Taipei and look forward to next year's show.
insight: B2B focus signals a growing importance of enterprise tech solutions.

title: Jensen Huang says 'every edge device will become autonomous' Nvidia maps one computing pattern from the cloud to robotics
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang foresees a future where every edge device becomes autonomous, with a new computing pattern that maps data from the cloud to robotics.
insight: Edge devices will increasingly rely on autonomous cloud-connected computing.

title: Meta putting up tents across the US to house AI servers, like a scene out of the movie Mad Max structures take three months to build and use jet engines for power
summary: Meta is rapidly building AI server tents across the US, cutting construction time from two to three years to just a few months. These structures are powered by jet engines, allowing for independence from the grid. The move aims to support Meta's growing data center needs.
insight: Meta's rapid expansion empowers its AI infrastructure development.

title: Outlook may have allowed unencrypted connections for decades, report claims Fedora and Dovecot upgrade reveal protocol downgrade issue present since at least 2007
summary: A report claims that Outlook may have allowed unencrypted connections since at least 2007. An upgrade to Fedora and Dovecot revealed a protocol downgrade issue. This issue may have compromised customer data security.
insight: Customer data security may have been compromised for over 15 years.

title: Lightning strike enters apartment through coaxial internet cable, blows up gamer's PC surge causes extensive damage to motherboard, destroys router, and leaves burn marks on the wall
summary: A lightning strike through a coaxial cable caused extensive damage to a gamer's PC and surrounding area. Proper grounding and protection can reduce the risk of costly hardware damage from lightning strikes. Direct lightning strikes can be difficult to defend against.
insight: Grounding and protection reduce lightning strike damage risks significantly.

title: Indiana mayor secretly recorded saying AI data center protestors only live in 'sh***y' houses office issues statement of clarification over controversial comments
summary: Shelbyville mayor Scott Ferguson made controversial comments about protestors' living conditions, sparking outrage.
insight: Elected officials' comments can reflect and perpetuate societal biases and inequalities.

title: Take your OpenClaw box back to the future with retro Mac Mini, Mac Studio docks Wokyis tempts Nintendo and Apple lawyers, while adding a screen, ports, and style to your modern Mac
summary: Wokyis is releasing a retro-themed Mac dock with larger screens and high throughput, offering a nostalgic twist on modern Macs. The M5 dock is already available, and the company plans to release a G7 NES-themed dock. The dock adds a screen, ports, and style to the Mac Mini or Mac Studio.
insight: Reviving nostalgia with tech innovation appeals to retro gaming enthusiasts

title: AMD's Frank Azor pushes back against claim that FSR 4.1 won't be ported to RDNA 3.5 GPUs says 'no such decision' has been made
summary: AMD's Frank Azor denies FSR 4.1 will be skipped on RDNA 3.5 GPUs.
insight: AMD's GPU compatibility matters for gamers' upgrade flexibility.

title: 8GB of RAM is back on laptops companies are lowering memory offerings to make affordable notebooks during component crisis
summary: Laptop manufacturers are reverting to 8GB RAM due to component crisis, aiming to make affordable notebooks. Dell and Acer introduced systems starting with 8GB of RAM to compete with the MacBook Neo. This move follows a recent trend of increasing RAM capacity to bolster AI.
insight: Budget-friendly laptops may compromise performance due to reduced RAM.

title: TSMC CEO C.C. Wei says, It will be a long time before we can meet customer demand tells shareholders that he will keep prices stable, refrain from implementing price hikes
summary: TSMC struggles to meet AI hyperscalers' demand, citing insufficient capacity. CEO C.C. Wei assures stable prices, despite high demand. This creates an opportunity for Intel to gain market share.
insight: Intel gains market share due to TSMC's capacity constraints.

title: Qualcomm Roundtable Interview transcript SVP of Compute and Gaming talks Snapdragon C, RTX Spark, and the agentic AI future
summary: Qualcomm's Snapdragon C targets the low-cost laptop market, while also building an AI ecosystem on its silicon. The company is expanding its offerings to compete in the growing laptop market and drive innovation in AI technology. It aims to establish a presence in the affordable and premium laptop segments.
insight: Qualcomm's agentic AI ecosystem shifts laptop tech paradigm

title: Nintendo Switch 2 with user-replaceable batteries coming to the EU console maker confirms it will comply with regulations set to take effect from 2027
summary: Nintendo will release an updated Switch 2 with user-replaceable batteries in the EU. The new console complies with regulations set to take effect from February 18, 2027. This change aims to reduce electronic waste.
insight: Compliance with EU regulations will reduce electronic waste significantly.

title: US tech layoffs record single-highest month in two years, and more than any other sector nearly 40,000 get the axe, AI the most cited reason for layoffs
summary: US tech companies announced 38,242 job cuts in May, exceeding all sectors and a nearly two-year high.
insight: Massive tech layoffs signal industry shift and potential AI disruption.

title: Hyte shows off Y50 chassis, aesthetic cable accessory kit, new fans, and updates Nexus Software sub $100 Y50 brings value to Y-series, Nexus 3.0 goes web-based, now works on Mac, Linux, Windows, and your phone
summary: Hyte showcased the Y50 chassis, aesthetic cable accessory kit, and new fans. They also updated Nexus Software, making it web-based and cross-platform. The Y50's value is now comparable to the Y-series at a sub-$100 price point.
insight: Hyte expands Y-series value at an affordable price point.

title: Best of Computex 2026: Innovating despite disruptions
summary: This year's Computex 2026 showcased innovative products including affordable premium laptops, next-gen handhelds, and Nvidia's Spark on Windows on Arm. The event highlighted advancements in technology despite disruptions in the industry. The introduction of new devices and technologies is set to shape the future of computing.
insight: Adaptation and innovation drive progress in the face of disruption.

title: Thundercomm TurboX C7790 Android and Linux development kit features Qualcomm Dragonwing Q-7790 Edge AI SoC
summary: Thundercomm launched the TurboX C7790 development kit, a compact edge AI platform based on the Qualcomm Dragonwing Q-7790 SoC, offering up to 24 TOPS of AI performance and supporting both Android and Linux operating systems.
insight: Enables efficient AI processing at the edge of networks

title: $54 ThinkNode G4 Wi-Fi HaLow gateway extends the range of Ethernet or 2.4 GHz WiFi 4 devices
summary: The ThinkNode G4 Wi-Fi HaLow gateway extends Ethernet or 2.4 GHz WiFi 4 device ranges using a Mediatek MT7628N processor and Morse Micro MM6108 Wi-Fi HaLow SoC.
insight: Long-range connectivity solution for IoT devices with low power consumption.

title: Erqos EQSP32CE An industrial IoT ESP32-S3 PLC with Ethernet, RS232, RS485, CAN Bus, DIN Rail support
summary: The Erqos EQSP32CE is a compact industrial IoT PLC offering extensive connectivity options and interfaces for versatile automation applications.
insight: Industrial automation gets a compact, connected, and versatile PLC solution.

title: EKOS An ESP32-S3 ePaper dashboard housed in an oak-aluminum enclosure (Crowdfunding)
summary: EKOS is a local-first ePaper dashboard built around ESP32-S3 SoC, offering full privacy and control without cloud dependency, in a repair-friendly oak-aluminum enclosure with two variants: Pure and Sense.
insight: Private, local control for smart home devices with no cloud dependency required.

title: NVIDIA Jetson gets fanless solid-state cooling module
summary: YPlasma has developed a DBD plasma cooling module for NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano, providing fanless solid-state cooling.
insight: Fanless cooling technology advances efficiency and reliability in embedded systems.

title: The RAM crisis is resurrecting DDR4 memory and motherboard production as hardware cycle reverses
summary: DDR4 memory and motherboard production are being ramped up due to increased demand, as DRAM and NAND shortages make DDR5 RAM unaffordable. Several manufacturers are preparing to increase production in response to the crisis. This shift is part of a hardware cycle reversal.
insight: Supply chain shortages force consumers to opt for older, more affordable tech.

title: Nvidia RTX 5000 Super series tipped for 2026 launch despite memory crisis
summary: Nvidia RTX 5000 Super series is reportedly set for a 2026 launch, despite memory concerns.
insight: Potential new GPU release may alleviate existing graphics card shortages.

title: ESA report finds 212 million Americans now play video games weekly
summary: The ESA report finds 212 million Americans play video games weekly, shedding the 'nerdy' reputation. The 2026 Essential Facts report draws on data from over 13,500 respondents. The study paints a detailed portrait of the US video game industry.
insight: Mainstream America has fully adopted video games as a popular form of entertainment

title: Spammers are flooding Reddit with fake posts designed to show up in AI search results
summary: Spammers are flooding Reddit with fake posts to influence AI search results, targeting companies selling peptides and hormone replacement therapy. They strategically post in forums to shape content used by AI models. This tactic exploits Reddit's growing role as a source for AI tools.
insight: AI model accuracy suffers from biased, manipulated online content.

title: What is the most commonly used password?
summary: The world's most commonly used password is easily crackable in under a second, highlighting a significant security risk.
insight: Weak passwords compromise online security and put sensitive data at risk.

title: SpaceX IPO could be the largest in history at a $1.76 trillion valuation
summary: SpaceX plans to go public with a $1.76 trillion valuation, potentially shattering records.
insight: Historic IPO could reshape the global tech and finance landscape

title: Android can now detect when scammers are faking calls from your contacts
summary: Google's Fake Call Detection feature helps Android users identify potential scam calls.
insight: Prevents scammers from impersonating loved ones via fake phone calls.

title: Bots have officially overtaken humans on the internet
summary: Bots have surpassed humans in internet traffic, according to Cloudflare Radar, making up around 56% of all traffic.
insight: Rise of bots threatens data accuracy and user experience online.

title: PC shipments could drop 20% by year's end as AI datacenters consume 70% of high-end DRAM
summary: PC shipments may decline by 20% due to supply shortages, mainly high-end DRAM. The shortage is caused by AI data centers consuming a large portion of high-end DRAM. This diversion of resources affects PC manufacturers.
insight: Supply chain diversion hurts PC industry, not AI data centers.

title: After 33 years, EA's College Football game is coming to PC for the first time
summary: EA's College Football game is coming to PC after 33 years, following a formal announcement.
insight: Historic PC release will expand game's reach and audience possibilities.

title: 71% of Americans now oppose data centers near their homes, up from 42% just nine months ago
summary: A survey by Heatmap Pro found 71% of Americans oppose data centers near their homes.
insight: Growing public opposition to data centers near residential areas.

title: Microsoft Office 2019 for Mac will no longer edit documents after July 13
summary: Microsoft Office 2019 for Mac will stop editing documents after July 13. Users with non-updated operating systems will be affected. Updating the OS and Office can resolve the issue.
insight: Older Office versions and OS updates are crucial for document compatibility.

title: Intel denies Arc desktop GPUs are going away
summary: Intel denies plans to discontinue Arc desktop graphics cards, calling them a crucial part of their PC lineup.
insight: Arc GPUs remain a vital component of Intel's PC offerings.

title: A truck-mounted nuclear reactor that runs for decades without refueling is being tested in China
summary: China is testing a truck-mounted nuclear reactor that runs for decades without refueling.
insight: Self-sustaining mobile nuclear power could revolutionize remote energy access worldwide.
